briefs.title: RxBrain: An Embodied AI Model That Reasons, Predicts, and Acts
briefs.description: A new foundation model unifies language reasoning with visual imagination for embodied cognition, enabling agents to connect high-level planning with physical state grounding.
briefs.why_it_matters: RxBrain eliminates the need for brittle modular pipelines by combining abstract task planning and physical state prediction in a single model, paving the way for more coherent and capable autonomous agents.
briefs.who_cares: Robotics engineers, embodied AI researchers, multimodal foundation model developers, and teams building autonomous agents for real-world deployment.

briefs.title: SUFLECA: One-Shot CAD-to-Image Alignment for Robotics and AR
briefs.description: A zero-shot method that replaces appearance-driven matching with geometry-grounded features to estimate 9D object poses from a single RGB image in under a second.
briefs.why_it_matters: SUFLECA achieves occlusion-robust pose estimation that outperforms fully supervised methods on ScanNet25k, enabling scalable, label-free CAD alignment for robotics and AR/VR applications.
briefs.who_cares: Robotics perception engineers, AR/VR developers, CAD alignment toolkit maintainers, and researchers in geometric deep learning.

briefs.title: Locality Over Globality: Biologically Inspired Vision Models
briefs.description: A study demonstrating that local, sequential visual processing enables better compositional generalization than global, single-shot vision models.
briefs.why_it_matters: This work challenges the dominant paradigm in computer vision, suggesting that biologically inspired architectures could solve fundamental limitations in current vision models by avoiding brittle shortcuts.
briefs.who_cares: Computer vision researchers, AI engineers designing vision-language models, agent developers working on embodied AI, and platform teams optimizing for scalable vision tasks.

briefs.title: AsySplat: Efficient 3D Scene Modeling for Real-Time Applications
briefs.description: A new asymmetric 3D Gaussian Splatting model that decouples geometry and appearance tasks, reducing redundant computation for long-sequence novel view synthesis.
briefs.why_it_matters: AsySplat slashes computational overhead while maintaining high-quality rendering, enabling real-time 3D scene modeling on resource-constrained hardware.
briefs.who_cares: 3D computer vision engineers, real-time graphics developers, AR/VR platform teams, and researchers focused on efficient radiance field rendering.

briefs.title: VIABench: A New Benchmark for Assistive AI in the Real World
briefs.description: A comprehensive video benchmark created by and for blind individuals, evaluating MLLMs on proactive, real-time assistance tasks.
briefs.why_it_matters: VIABench exposes critical gaps in current assistive AI by shifting evaluation from generic vision tasks to real-world blind assistance, redefining benchmarks for accessibility-focused models.
briefs.who_cares: AI researchers in multimodal models, assistive tech developers, robotics engineers for navigation systems, and accessibility-focused product teams.
